# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

# Use the full Ubuntu image as the base
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=off \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=on \
    POETRY_VIRTUALENVS_CREATE=false

# Update apt and install Python, pip, and various development tools
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    python3.10 \
    python3-pip \
    curl \
    wget \
    git \
    vim \
    build-essential \
    libpq-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libffi-dev \
    openssh-client \
    sudo \
    && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a symlink for python3 to just 'python' and pip3 to 'pip'
RUN update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/python python /usr/bin/python3.10 1 && \
    update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/pip pip /usr/bin/pip3 1

# Install Poetry for dependency management (optional, but good for data science)
RUN pip install poetry

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the application code
COPY . /app

# Install application dependencies using poetry (if poetry.lock exists)
# Or install using pip from requirements.txt
# Example for poetry:
# COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
# RUN poetry install --no-dev --no-root
#
# Example for pip:
# COPY requirements.txt ./
# RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Define default command (e.g., if you have a main script)
# CMD ["python", "your_data_processing_script.py"]
# Or just keep the container running for interactive use (useful for debugging)
CMD ["bash"]
